name: roundtrip_ptr_to_index_to_ptr_fails
code:
proof fn phi_1_roundtrip_ptr_to_index_to_ptr_fails(ptr: usize)
    requires
        ptr % 0x1000 == 0,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(spec_page_ptr2page_index(ptr)) != ptr,
{
}
reason: If ptr→index→ptr is not the identity on aligned pointers, the two conversions are inconsistent and page lookups would silently corrupt addresses.

name: roundtrip_index_to_ptr_to_index_fails
code:
proof fn phi_2_roundtrip_index_to_ptr_to_index_fails(i: usize)
    requires
        0 <= i < NUM_PAGES,
    ensures
        spec_page_ptr2page_index(spec_page_index2page_ptr(i)) != i,
{
}
reason: If index→ptr→index is not the identity for valid indices, page array indexing after a pointer round-trip would access the wrong page entry.

name: index2ptr_breaks_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_3_index2ptr_breaks_alignment(i: usize)
    requires
        0 <= i < NUM_PAGES,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) % 0x1000 != 0,
{
}
reason: If index-to-pointer conversion produces non-4k-aligned addresses, the result violates the precondition of page_ptr2page_index and cannot be used as a valid page pointer.

name: zero_ptr_maps_to_nonzero_index
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_ptr_maps_to_nonzero_index()
    requires
        0usize % 0x1000 == 0,
    ensures
        spec_page_ptr2page_index(0usize) != 0usize,
{
}
reason: If the zero address (trivially aligned) does not map to index 0, the base-case correspondence between the page array and physical memory is broken.

name: ptr2index_non_injective
code:
proof fn phi_5_ptr2index_non_injective(p1: usize, p2: usize)
    requires
        p1 % 0x1000 == 0,
        p2 % 0x1000 == 0,
        p1 != p2,
    ensures
        spec_page_ptr2page_index(p1) == spec_page_ptr2page_index(p2),
{
}
reason: If two distinct aligned pointers map to the same index, the page array cannot distinguish them, causing aliasing bugs where operations on one page silently affect another.
